review_title: Comprehensive benefits with concerns over operational balance and promotion fairness
review_body_text: I spent more than 10 years at Bank Islam and found the benefits to be quite comprehensive. The work culture is generally positive with supportive and fun colleagues, and the bank offers good flexibility through work-from-home arrangements for specific departments. However, there are significant challenges regarding work-life balance, particularly in operations where the workload is often overwhelming. Since 2021, the bonus payouts (averaging 2 months) have not felt commensurate with the level of effort required. Furthermore, the promotion structure appears to be heavily influenced by seniority and favoritism rather than clear meritocracy, which can be discouraging for high-performers. Additionally, top management often feels disconnected from ground-level operational reality. While it is a stable organization, the compensation-to-effort ratio and career progression transparency need improvement.

review_title: Very heavy workloads, no work life balance & toxic management
review_body_text: '- I always saw my colleagues were always working during their annual leaves and weekends in the office for the whole day due to strict deadlines set by ISO regulations - My mentor even asked me to cancel my annual leaves to complete my workloads, and my mentor asked me to complete my workloads during my annual leaves, weekends and public holiday despite unrealistic goals set by the boss and the team. - Your workload will be increased without letting you know in advance or without asking your permission to do so. - Your colleagues will punish you with extra workloads if you didn't complete their KPI (number of cases to be completed per day) - Even if you have a little progress within 4 months, the boss and my mentor won't be happy or celebrate your progress, the only thing they do is by forcing you to do faster with increasing amount of workloads. - My mentor has very biased treatment, my mentor is always friendly towards the other colleagues (who are close to my mentor), but when comes to my male colleague (in same department) and me as a new worker, my mentor always give very bad compliment or judge us in a biased way. My mentor also reacted excessively on trivial things about me and controlled me on every movement ("Don't show that you are not happy, because I saw you that you banged the table with documents", while the actual event: The documents are too heavy that I can't carry properly, hence I have to arrange the documents with accidental bang). My mentor also talked bad about male colleague behind his back when male colleague took MC due to his illness.

review_title: A good company to start with in banking line
review_body_text: Working at Public Bank Berhad can be a rewarding yet demanding experience, especially in the fast-paced environment of a commercial bank. Like most banks, the workload can be overwhelming, with employees often expected to handle multiple responsibilities, tight deadlines, and high-performance targets. Long working hours are common, particularly in departments such as sales, credit analysis, and customer service. However, this is considered the norm in the banking industry, where efficiency and accuracy are crucial. One of the key factors that can make the experience more enjoyable is having supportive and competent teammates. A good team can help ease the workload, provide guidance, and create a positive work environment, making it easier to manage stress. Public Bank is known for its structured work culture, and employees who can adapt to its disciplined and customer-focused approach tend to succeed. Overall, while the job can be challenging, it offers stability, career growth opportunities, and valuable banking experience. Employees who thrive under pressure and enjoy teamwork may find Public Bank a good place to build a long-term career.
Maybank is a good place for fresh graduate, a lot you can learn and develop here. However, there is no work life balance that may make you exhausted. Need to always on standby mode.

Poor management and leadership, with a lack of recognition for employees who go above and beyond their regular duties. During bonus and increment cycles, staff handling only BAU tasks often receive higher total compensation than those who take on additional responsibilities. Moreover, there is a lack of transparency in the compensation distribution process. Favoritism from both leadership and HRBP influences career growth, resulting in the same individuals receiving promotions every 2–3 years, while others are overlooked. Despite implementing 360-degree feedback and the Employee Engagement Survey (EES), employee concerns remain unaddressed. The work environment is highly political and toxic, with poor work-life balance. The recent acquisition of Citi’s business appears to have been poorly managed, leading to a hiring freeze post-merger. As a result, employees are now handling workloads far beyond their capacity, yet compensation remains minimal, with low increments and bonuses. While it is understandable that the bank must demonstrate profitability to shareholders, it is crucial to acknowledge the efforts of employees who drive this success.

Public sector banks are known for offering stable and attractive salary packages, making them a popular choice for individuals seeking long-term career opportunities. While the salary structure varies based on the position and level of experience, public bank employees typically enjoy competitive pay scales that are often revised based on recommendations from government pay commissions. A key advantage is the inclusion of various allowances such as housing rent allowance (HRA), dearness allowance (DA), and special allowances. In addition to the fixed salary, public bank employees also receive additional perks like medical benefits, pension schemes, and paid leave. Bonuses and annual increments are also part of the compensation structure, making it financially rewarding over time. The salary in public banks may not be as high as in private sector banks initially, but the long-term financial security, work-life balance, and other benefits make public banks a desirable option for many. Additionally, public bank employees often have more structured career paths, including promotional avenues that lead to higher pay grades and responsibilities.

My time at CIMB has unfortunately been less than satisfactory. While there are some positives, such as a supportive team and a convenient location, the overall experience has been marred by significant shortcomings. One of the most glaring issues is the lack of effective management. There seems to be a disconnect between upper management's vision and the day-to-day operations, leading to confusion and a sense of aimlessness among employees. This has resulted in inefficiencies and missed opportunities for improvement. Additionally, the work-life balance at CIMB leaves much to be desired. The workload can be overwhelming, often requiring employees to work long hours with little acknowledgment or compensation. This imbalance has taken a toll on morale and productivity. Furthermore, opportunities for career development are scarce. There is a noticeable absence of structured programs or support for advancing one's skills and career within the company. In conclusion, while there are some positive aspects to working at CIMB, such as a supportive team environment and convenient location, the overall experience has been subpar due to ineffective management, poor work-life balance, and limited career development opportunities. Prospective employees should carefully weigh these factors before considering employment here..

review_title: Fast paced but try to connect with competent seniors/colleagues
review_body_text: 1. You need to have communication skills including negotiation, patience, and listening skills to assist the customers with their inquiries outstandingly. Customers are generally normal and sane. Regardless they might rant because they are having difficulties. Just assist, advise, and resolve. 2. Systems are numerous. Getting used to its interface. Your daily tasks may help you to improve how to utilize them. Also, please give ample time to catch up and get used to the systems even though OIC might not assist you many times. Some OICs did good things when they willingly shared notes about raising the service request. You just need to refer. 3. The 1-month training class might not be enough, the notes you can't gather from outside of class. So for those who want to revise, please jot down clearly inside your notebook. Once you hit the floor, you only can refer to their Infohub system or your simplified notes. You try to focus on the necessary topic first. Ask your trainer the most common inquiries about EACH topic. Because trust me, you can't remember 100% from class to call floor later on. 3. OICs might be helping you but they will expect you to work independently, and catch up so fast. Sometimes, they might get irritated when you ask twice because they think they already explained thousands of times. Perhaps the same questions also come from your colleagues. So for those who are not familiar with banking terms/products, if your background is not related to banking/financials, you have to learn yourself. This one is supposed to be okay, but if you need them and they don't entertain willingly, definitely that will let you down. 4. Systems are fine except I only encounter difficulties with R*W**, it needs to be redesigned to a friendlier interface at least for those agents. I think it's too chaotic and tedious. Just need to make it readable so that easier to answer the enquiries instead of arranging a callback because you usually need more time to extract the info. 5. No work-life balance, kindly expect this as you need to perform follow-ups. But, as recommended by OICs/seniors, try to multitask by submitting the SR while talking with customers. Either listen to rants and do your job, or just put them on hold to submit the SR, both have been practiced there. You can choose to claim OT as well at the end of the shift to submit the SR but extra works that you have to get validation from your OICs or TM. Despite this being normal, they needed to check first. 6. I realized that OICs might have different understandings. So well equipped yourself with knowledge, and practiced to rely on many OICs/TMs for the same inquiries. You will notice to get different answers. 7. The job is fun, especially for those who like to talk to people and assist them. I wanted to stay but I felt disappointed for a couple of reasons, which made me resign. But rest assured, if you have REALLY good support and fun colleagues, you actually can survive.

Working at Bank Rakyat as a contract executive for two years was a valuable and enriching experience. Despite the occasional stress, the knowledge gained in the field of financing was immense. The supportive and friendly nature of my colleagues significantly contributed to making the overall work environment positive. One notable aspect of the job was the yearly bonuses, which were exceptionally good. This not only served as a motivating factor but also reflected the organization's commitment to recognizing and rewarding the hard work of its employees. The acceptable overtime policy allowed for a healthy work-life balance, and the collaborative atmosphere within the bank made the workload more manageable. One of the highlights of my time at Bank Rakyat was the willingness of my colleagues to share their knowledge and expertise. The culture encouraged learning from one another, fostering a sense of teamwork and camaraderie. This collaborative approach helped me to navigate the complexities of the job more effectively and ensured a smoother integration into the organizational dynamics. Overall, my experience at Bank Rakyat was not only professionally rewarding but also personally fulfilling. The combination of a supportive work environment, generous bonuses, and the opportunity to learn from experienced colleagues made it a positive chapter in my career.

Title: Maybank - A Superb Workplace My experience at Maybank has been truly outstanding, and here's why it stands out as an exceptional workplace: 1. **Inclusive Culture**: Maybank fosters diversity and inclusion, creating a strong sense of belonging among employees. 2. **Supportive Management**: Maybank's leadership is approachable and values employee feedback. 3. **Work-Life Balance**: The company promotes work-life balance, offering flexibility and wellness support. 4. **Growth Opportunities**: Maybank invests in professional development and career advancement. 5. **Competitive Benefits**: They offer competitive compensation and a comprehensive benefits package. 6. **Innovation**: Maybank embraces innovation and technology, keeping the work dynamic. 7. **Community Engagement**: Maybank encourages community involvement, giving employees a sense of purpose. 8. **Collaborative Environment**: Teamwork is a priority, creating a positive and productive atmosphere. 9. **Modern Facilities**: The workplace is comfortable, modern, and well-equipped. 10. **Job Security**: Maybank provides stability and financial security. In summary, Maybank is a community of passionate individuals working together to achieve common goals. If you seek a workplace that values employees, promotes growth, and makes a positive impact, Maybank is the place to be.

review_title: Rigid corporate structure, top to down management style
review_body_text: Outdated management style. Top management inflexible when it comes to opinion from junior. End result is younger talents will likely jump ship while those who just follow orders passively will be rewarded or promoted by the top management. I was hired with contract because this job is high turnover, no wonder lo. If you didn't not finish the contract time, you will be charge Rm2500 to pay to them as the training bond. The job was so stressing your mind. Everyday have to hear complaints which is the part of the job. If you priorities your mental health, I do not recommend this job. They have the kpi you need to meet, they always keep telling you to shorten the time call but you need to wrap in CRM. It so stressful. If the queries still pending and appeal, you still need to keep on updates where we have many calls that we tend to forget. I got anxiety because always intimidate with the calls. If you handle chat or email, I still consider, but if inbound or outbound calls, think on your mental health. Pros- if you achieve target on that day, they will treat the meal. They provide free beverage vending machine which provide you coffee, cordial and refreshing beverage. You have 1 hour break. Then 30 minutes extra. You have to sacrifice your time because some shift can end at 1am, and start at 6am. Salary is high for fresh graduate which is 2.7k. Your manager is so supportive and understanding, but the call environment too stressful. 4 minutes from lrt ampang Park, which in located at intermark mall.

The salary is good for fresh graduates. However the there are too much workload and follow up need to be done after end shift. Too often I need to stay back after end shift to complete work amd cannot claim OT as it only count if taking calls.
High responsibilities Learning new things Thinking out of box Experiencing handling foreign bankers Working hours is very sufficient Many benefits were offered to staff

Nothing much to do because it was pandemic period and it was also towards end of the year when colleagues were starting to clear their leaves, and a few caught covid and everyone had to self-quarantine at home. In the end the work i was given to do were mostly boring admin work, even when you ask other colleagues for work they said they had nothing for me. Couldn’t learn much from anyone. But everyone was generally nice and friendly.

Best place to work. The environment is very healthy and got so much support from colleagues and managers. Eventhough there's challenges in doing sales, but when there's team spirit, you will be able to achieve everything

High volumes Seniors are unwilling to teach & help. Managers - some old folks who is bad with computer (don't even know how to use excel...print out reports to check & use long ruler to check the alignment...) Gosh...

review_title: They have a very bad work life balance for any job that is in the Hire Purchase Department unless you are applying for a clerk position
review_body_text: They hope that you work more than the scheduled work hours and they do not compensate for OT, and the management (Hire Purchase Department) hate to see that their workers go back on time even if you have completed your work you still need to act busy and then go back at 8pm or 9 pm (schedule working hours are 8.45am - 5.45pm). If you think that bank benefits are good, you are wrong too, the benefits are all the same with any established companies just the extra thing is that if you work with them you will easily get loans from them, but then again they're pay is not high and the loan benefit is just basically paying back to the people who pays you to work there, and they receive your money with interest, is a win-win situation for them. You will get special housing loan rate with them but if you quit your job your housing interest will increase back to any normal people's interest rate. Bonuses and increment, bonus for HP department is minimum 2 months basic pay, but if you take those 2 months Bonuses and divided by 12 months and you calculate that you work an extra hour every single day, is not even enough to say that that bonuses paid your extra one hour (OT) you worked for that year. Is less then 1.5 times of the OT rate. But considering that they wish you to work until 8 pm, the bonus is not really a bonus anymore if you see it this way, you can be paid more if a company actually pays for OT. They have a performance bonuses is separately paid from the 2 months minimum bonus, this performance bonus can reach up to 6 months pay,only special cases will get 6 month bonuses and this employee actually hit close to 200% or more than 200% of his annual target, the mean average is people achieving their target is around 80% to 100% plus, you will only given this bonus if only you hit more than 100% of your target. Increment is based on KPI and there is no transparency on the calculations of your increments, they always says is up to management to calculate the increment.

review_body_text: I’am currently serving as a contract clerk in one of the Regional Collection Centre Branches. Yes, the work enviorment is very relaxed becuse 85% of the employees in the office are family people and senior citizens whom have serviced not less than 5 years with the bank. Communication among staffs and management are good but somehow there will be always 1-2 senior staffs whom disagrees everything the boss does or said (very strong in employee rights/unions stuff). Other than that, since its a 100% Malay culture, laziness is there but the act of good in properous meal ‘belanja’ always being done weekly by any of the kind-hearted staffs other than staffs are helpfull among each other. Salary wise for a diploma range contract staff is kind off sad for its figure but atleast the annual bonuses, ex-gratia payouts and claims are elidgable (as per permanent staffs) for service not less than 6 months on the day of the bonus announcement date via email. Even so, in day 2 of reporting, one of the staff recommended me to apply Staff Credit Card (I never had any credit card before) and guess what? They approved for a handsome limit of the card compared to my salary 😂. In conclusion, working with Bank Rakyat is fun and nice place to work just like any other conpanies but the con’s is always there and it depends on how you deal with it professionally.
pros_text: Free lunches (belanja by bosses and senior staffs on a monthly basis), Good staff credit card apporoval limits, Unlimited claims (as long your pengurus cawangan acknowledges it), Staff hotel benefit rates for holidays, Annual Bonuses, Ex-Gratia Payouts, Claimable Medical Bills (certain minor cases), Very religious islamic Staffs and Helpfull staffs
cons_text: Certain staffs are lazy, Kaki kipas staffs, Low salary start up for contracted staffs

review_title: Stressful work environment but the reward/benefits given are great
review_body_text: During my tenure at Public Bank, I am part of Hire Purchase Credit Processing HP Centre. With team of 10 Officers, each of us need to process an average of 10 cases per day and was given 3 days to submit a decision whether the customer submission approved or rejected. It's very stressful as the pace of new case submission was very fast and It need me to go back home late everyday. Different department has different challenges. Like Housing Loan Department are less stressful as officer part only on customer credit evaluation. The rest will be done by panel lawyer as it involves government agencies. For Frontline Counter they are facing Queue Management System (QMS) which HQ will track how many customers are waiting for their number to be call in more than 2 minutes. But the best part in Public Bank, of course the bonus and ex-gratia. The management are good in taking care staff welfare. Medical benefit are given to staff and their families, 2 months bonus are given to all staff every year and in addition special ex-gratia based on staff performance up to 5 months and low interest rate 0% - 2% for staff housing loan. Overall, although work environment are stressful but working in Public Bank build my personality in terms of work wise fast and accurate, customer handling and evaluation, useful experience, etc
location: Public Bank Taman Molek HP Centre JB

review_title: Exciting opportunities to interact with various stakeholders
review_body_text: A typical day begins at 8.45 a.m. by employees checking into office and signing their name on the register book. An employee would start going through client files and make a list of important appointments and requests in order to establish priorities and enhance workflow efficiency and effectiveness. In between, the employee may initiate or receive communication from the client on matters involving banking services and products. Around lunch hour, depending on the level of activity in the office, the employee could arrange for a lunch meeting with colleagues or clients who are seeking additional facilities or information on term deposit rates. The cycle then repeats until after lunch where the employee continues to review business cases and address various events at they come from both internal and external stakeholder groups.

Thus, I have to constantly observe the customers and entertain to their enquires in the ways that would make them feel good about my service and make them feel they came to search for answers, they got their answers.

Work with RHB is good experience for me because RHB is a big company and have their own culture that provide employer love work with RHB. They provide us a lot of training to make us more ready to real business banking and always ready to help customers. They also have a good management. The hardest part of the job is we try to give our best for our target. Even our target is high but we are ready to give our best to achieved the targets. The most enjoyable part of the job is our family cultures. we always support each other to make it our work place no pressure.

Daily routine in Public Bank is to evaluate customers' profile and liaise with different branches to get more customers' information before making lending decision. Co-workers are friendly and we helped each other while working. The most enjoyable part of the job is when challenges happens such as when customers' provided fraud documents, it is our job to detect the fraud and when there is new systems to be tested.

review_body_text: The best experience while working in maybank when our KPI performance is either on target or exceed target. Meaning we delivered our tasks in timely manner or performance rating could be better than higher management expectation. The reason why bonus will be paid based on rating percentage and key performance in our KPI. Our employer never failed to pay bonus to entire staff except for those who didn't perform well. In additional, we also will be getting an ESOS, equity shareholders. Meaning all Maybankers beneficial will be entitled an ESOS offer depends on their level or position. The higher position is the better ESOS offered. Medical benefits is the most important and the most valuable HR benefit for Maybankers. It will covers a staff and also a family members where it won't be as same as with any other sectors of financial institution or company locally.
pros_text: Good to work in permanently because of HR benefits

I need to archive monthly Kpi every month as well. -every agent have their Team Leader(TL) or Team Manager. which is will guide and monitor our work productivity. -everyone knows that customer service is stress job especially with financial institution(bank) but environment and colleagues was help me a lot while working. this company have some event like dinner, hiking, aidilfitri event and other. its make us have good relationship not even with same level person in this company -hardest part in this job was i am working with different from my studies. far from any subject in my courses in university. but all this things only hard in the early of my carrier. i showed and proved that if people get a chance they will struggle and committed on any difficult task. same goes with jobs experiences. without chance we cant get experience -lastly, most enjoyable part for sure was satisfaction from customer after can settled and solved problem. include compliment from cust.

But in maybank... the promotion is limited for each branch every year . So if you want something fastrack for career, you definately wont easily get . As many of senior staff waiting for long.

review_title: Quite tough job however nice team members make us release the stress
review_body_text: The job scope of sales executive is not only to get a case, develop relationship networks with dealers and customers to market the hire purchase, we need to follow up each stage of the case from the day the case assign until the disbursement of the case. I have learned how to assign the case, submission to hq, processing (CCRIS, calculation of the interest rate, EIR, DSR, CRR, guarantor and so on), LOU, e-hakmilik, payment, disbursement and so on. The hardest part of the job is to build a new relationship and dealing with dealers and customers. In addition, with the current economics problem, the market was very slow, so quite tough to survive. However, the most enjoyable part of the job is when we can give the best services to our customers and make them satisfied with us.

review_title: A boring place to work but alot of benefits

contract job learned about management & customer service skills lots of friends most challengge part : to make customer feel secured with bank & we understand their problem

Having worked in the Bank for almost 40 years, I would say it was like my second home. Depending on which unit you work with, it can be very busy and hectic. If I worked in the telecommunication centre where we receive messages from all over the world and having different time zones, I begin my day early and rushing to sort out incoming messages and also very hectic towards end of the day when messages have to go out. We have great teamwork and work with a great sence of urgency with my colleagues. The hardest part was when trade export documents go missing, resulting in importers not being able to get hold of their goods and incurring demurrage charges. It was great to have our co-workers who were helpful and hadworking.
Good place to work, good benefits to staff, environment was also good, and the most enjoy part of the job is the management give appreciation that satisfied the staff.

The hardest part is achieving monthly target. Since this is team-work based, everybody needs to achieve their personal and also team target. review_title: Challenging workforce withroom for growth and career progression
review_body_text: Daily task include analysing market opportunities to come up with product ideas that suits the market trend and customer's needs and preferences. With this, I have learn to adapt to the changing market environment and work under pressure as a split second would cost a lost of opportunity or money position for the clients. My managers : Margaret, Steven and Mr Khoo had been really supportive when it comes to product approvals and recognition of one's efforts. I have been generously rewarded for the handwork that I have shown and this enhances and boost my morale values. I work with a team of 10 other people who carries the same role. We each exhibit a strong cooperation system and there's a buddy system in tact which enables one to have a back up if any unforeseen circumstances prevail. The hardest part of the job is when we have a bad day at the market, where most indices dropped or the currencies are taking a beating, it is the mental and emotional position of our clients that we would need to manage eg. being shouted at or even enduring tantrums and anger hurled at you and our job would be to pacify the clients and offer alternative solutions. The most enjoyable part would definitely be monetary. Being an employee on both basic salary and monthly incentives, the money is the pull factor. I am accountable for what I bring home extra every month and when you do your best, the satisfaction is there when you bring home a big fat paycheque. I have learn a lot working in UOB, a lot of opportunities was given to me. Steven in particular was nurturing me and guiding me for a managerial role, I was exposed to all the numerical and analytical tasks and meetings and presentations to help me understand the business better and how to run a treasury advisory team, ensuring we are using our team budget accordingly. It was definitely something that made me realise my talent and the opportunity to learn.

review_title: Summer Internship in CIMB Investment Bank
review_body_text: I was an intern in the Equity capital markets department, CIMB Investment Bank, Malaysia for a duration of two months in early 2013. I was involved in the Ranhill IPO, whereby I helped to create a teaser of the IPO for presentation to cornerstone investors, describing the company's strengths, strategies, industry dynamics and growth prospects. Not only that, I also assisted with creation, editing, and analysis of documents by compelling industry and company data, performing basic financial analysis, and creating presentation documents. Being involved in prospectus launches for various IPOs allowed me to understand corporate finance better. Although the working hours were long, I enjoyed learning about the fusion of marketing and finance.

review_title: A good ground to learn from operation to management level
review_body_text: There is no typical day as such, but monitoring the current trust, tax and estate planning development on a weekly basis is vital. Every day is different: one day you might be out seeing clients, perhaps reviewing their personal situation or entertaining them with a day at the golf tournament. The next day you could be in the office, documenting transactions, drumming up new business and dealing with clients by a face to face meeting. My clients are in the range of high net worth and ultra high net worth. They won't consume their wealth in their lifetimes, and they tend to be extremely conservative and careful when passing down their wealth to the next generations. I'm like a doctor on call 24/7. We're dealing with the wealthiest people in the world … if they call you up and ask you a question, and you're not prepared, your credibility and the credibility of the Company is hurt. Once they trust you, they're our best source of new clients and it is really a good and satisfied feeling when these happens.

As people know, CIMB Investment Bank is adamant to become the biggest and successful South East Asia's M&A and corporate finance business including Hong Kong, China, and Australia. Apart from investment banking, CIMB commercial banking already set a foothold in every country in South East Asia. Very recent opening was Myanmar and Phillipines. Thus, embracing the expansion is easy to say than done. As part of CIMB Investment Bank, my team also also has to play a role. Understanding the management's load of work can be very helpful. With the experience i have had during my stint in several local securities firm, i manage to keep the workflow smoothly without interfering much on his workload where the bottom line will explain everything, of course with god's will. So i did learn when client database is getting bigger or whenever i foresee big trading volume, getting the client unsatisfied is the last thing you want to do. In the otherwise, by ensuring them satisfaction, every level in the management will be very happy in what i do. While my co-worker are team oriented, it will add more value when i keep guiding them and at the same time take guidance from the experience one. I dont know where this people will end up later, so by giving them my best is my best bet. My hardest part of the job is working in odd hours (handling U.S and U.K trades) and most enjoyable part of the job is the learning curve, good pays and good bonus, of course at god's will.

review_title: CIMB Bank Berhad can basically be considered a performance based company wherein salary and benefits are paid according to performance.
review_body_text: A day in CIMB Bank can easily be likened to that of a normal office wherein customer's are a priority. The day commences with calls to customers to either review existing facilities or market new products to existing or new customers. All staff are guided by Key Performance Indicators KPI) hence one is very much guided by these KPIs which are generally given out based on the individual's key role. To me, the hardest part of the job was to keep all customers satisfied and the most enjoyable part of my job can be said to be the part when a customer's loan is disbursed and the joy on the customer's face is enough to erase all the turbulent and trying times experienced with the customer, i.e at the onset from the loan application to the final step, i.e. the disbursement.
pros_text: low interest for loans, preferential fixed deposit rates, preferential remittance rates, medical benefits for staff & family members, friendly management
